question_title: Will any of these AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Mistral AI) have a funding round in July 2026?
summary: Question resolves YES if OpenAI, Anthropic, or Mistral has a TracXn funding round dated July 2026. Both OpenAI (Series F closed, S-1 filed) and Anthropic (Series H closed May, 'likely last private raise') are IPO-track with no pending private round — and critically, TracXn shows zero new dated entries for either in May or June 2026, indicating the mega-round tranche follow-ons have already stopped. Mistral's rumored ~€3B raise, only in early discussions as of mid-June with no lead investor or close, is the main path to a July entry but unlikely to finalize that fast. Building forward per-company (OpenAI ~7%, Anthropic ~7%, Mistral ~17%) gives ~29%, with a small uplift for TracXn dating lags and backfill noise, landing at ~0.34.

factors.description: Both OpenAI and Anthropic are IPO-track with closed 'final' pre-IPO rounds; TracXn shows zero new dated entries for either in May or June 2026 — the tranche follow-on cluster has already stopped.
Mistral has a live pending ~€3B raise (first reported June 12 2026) but in early discussions with no lead/close — a specific July close within ~6 weeks is possible but not established.

Boom-era historical monthly hit rates (Anthropic ~30%, OpenAI ~11%) elevate baseline, and TracXn logs small tranches/debt/grants/backfills as separate dated rows, adding noise that could yield a July entry.
Three of the world's most active fundraisers over a full month; low-probability tail of a fresh announcement or retroactively-dated entry.

reasoning:
My forecast: **0.34** (34%).

**Reasoning:** The question resolves YES if OpenAI, Anthropic, or Mistral has a TracXn funding round dated July 2026. Key findings:

- **OpenAI** and **Anthropic** have both closed what are described as their final pre-IPO rounds (OpenAI's Series F, S-1 filed ~May; Anthropic's Series H, announced late May). Critically, TracXn shows **no new dated entries for either in May or June 2026** — the mega-round tranche follow-on cluster from Jan–Apr has already stopped, undercutting the case that follow-ons would spill into July.
- **Mistral** is the only live catalyst — a rumored ~€3B raise first reported June 12, but only in early discussions with no lead investor or close as of early July. A specific July close within ~6 weeks is possible but unlikely.

Building forward per-company (OpenAI ~7%, Anthropic ~7%, Mistral ~17%) gives ~29%, with a small uplift for TracXn's dating lags and tendency to backfill small/tranche/debt entries — landing at **~34%**.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if any of these companies has a funding round listed by TracXn with a date in July 2026: [Mistral AI](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/mistral-ai/__SLZq7rzxLYqqA97jtPwO09jLDeb76RVJVb306OhciWU/funding-and-investors#funding-rounds),  [Anthropic](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/anthropic/__SzoxXDMin-NK5tKB7ks8yHr6S9Mz68pjVCzFEcGFZ08/funding-and-investors#funding-rounds), or [OpenAI](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/openai/__kElhSG7uVGeFk1i71Co9-nwFtmtyMVT7f-YHMn4TFBg/funding-and-investors).
fine_print: No other resolution source will be used in resolving this question.

factors.description: Historical TracXn monthly hit rate in boom era: Anthropic ~30% of months, OpenAI ~11% of months. Combined at-least-one from these two ~37% unconditional.
Jan-Apr 2026 was extremely active: 6 combined OpenAI+Anthropic dated entries over 8 company-months (~1.5/month), driven by mega-round tranche follow-ons. Boom environment ongoing.

Both OpenAI (Series F closed Feb-Mar, S-1 filed) and Anthropic (Series H closed May, 'likely last private raise') have completed their rounds and are IPO-track — no new private round pending, depressing near-term new entries.

Mistral has a live pending ~€3B raise first reported June 12 2026, in early discussions — realistic path to a July-dated entry, plus Anthropic Series H tranche follow-ons could bleed into June/July given TracXn dating lags.
TracXn logs small tranches, debt, grants, extensions as separate dated rows; three hyperactive companies make a single July entry plausible even absent a headline round.

input.anchor: ~45% combined historical monthly hit rate (Anthropic 30%, OpenAI 11%, Mistral ~15%) in current boom era
input.assessment: Question resolves YES if any of OpenAI/Anthropic/Mistral has a TracXn-listed funding round dated July 2026. Two of three (OpenAI, Anthropic) just closed what are described as final pre-IPO rounds, reducing new-round likelihood. Mistral's pending €3B round is the main live catalyst but was only in early talks mid-June. TracXn logs many small/tranche/debt entries and has dating lags, adding baseline noise. Boom era elevates frequency but the specific-month, post-mega-round timing is the key constraint. Balancing ~37% two-company historical base rate plus Mistral wildcard plus tranche-followon noise against the 'rounds just closed' depressant lands near coin-flip, slightly below.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor ~0.45. Factors roughly offset (boom + Mistral catalyst vs. rounds-just-closed). Final near coin-flip is genuine uncertainty, not lazy hedging — the two mega-rounds closing and one pending small round genuinely balance.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether Mistral's €3B closes/gets dated in July; whether Anthropic Series H spawns tranche follow-on entries dated June/July on TracXn; TracXn's exact dating conventions.
input.tool_audit: research() sub-agent returned strong specifics: current funding status of all three companies, TracXn listing behavior, and monthly base rates. AskNews ran out of credits so July 5-31 events not captured, but resolution is future anyway. No failures otherwise.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was highly effective here, delivering both the forward-looking funding status and the crucial base-rate/TracXn-behavior context in one batch. For this definitional/measurement-flavored question, the main irreducible uncertainty is TracXn's idiosyncratic dating, which no tool can fully resolve. Scaffolding was adequate.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified every factor against the research trace and TracXn funding tables captured in the trace. Factor grounding is solid across the board:

- Factor 1 (base rates ~30% Anthropic, ~11% OpenAI): grounded — trace states "rounds appear in ~11% of OpenAI's months, ~30% of Anthropic's months over full history."
- Factor 2 (6 combined OpenAI+Anthropic entries Jan–Apr 2026 / 8 company-months): confirmed by the actual TracXn tables in the trace — Anthropic dated entries Jan 27, Feb 12, Apr 21 2026; OpenAI Feb 27, Mar 20, Apr 22 2026.
- Factor 3 (both mega-rounds closed, IPO-track): grounded (OpenAI S-1 filed ~May 22; Anthropic Series H "likely last private raise").
- Factor 4 (Mistral €3B pending, first reported June 12, early discussions): grounded (TechCrunch/Bloomberg/MLQ sources).
- Factor 5 (TracXn logs tranches/debt/grants as separate dated rows): grounded (OpenAI Series F appears 3× including $40M and $75M tranche rows).

No hallucinations, no double-counting, no wrong-direction factors, no assessment contradiction. Resolution criteria are provided; the agent correctly engaged with the specific TracXn source and July-2026 timeframe. Worldview research entries (two, both "fresh") are consistent with the factors; no contradictory cross-question forecasts exist. Tool audit narrative ("no failures otherwise") is effectively accurate — the single "error" in ground truth is the premortem tool (this review), not a research failure; trivial.

My concern is the probability. The TracXn tables in the trace show the most recent dated entry for BOTH OpenAI and Anthropic is April 2026 — and despite the Series H being *announced* late May, TracXn dated it April. So there are two full months (May and June 2026) with ZERO new dated entries for either company, right after the mega-round cluster. This directly undercuts the "tranche follow-ons bleed into adjacent months" reasoning in Factors 2 and 5: the observed evidence is that the follow-ons already stopped by May. The agent's anchor (~45% combined monthly hit rate) sums per-company unconditional boom-era rates but does not condition on this observed post-round drought.

Building forward from published_at: OpenAI (no pending round, IPO-track, 2-month drought) ~6-8% for July; Anthropic (same) ~6-8%; Mistral (€3B in "early discussions" mid-June, no lead/close as of July 4, mega-rounds take weeks-to-months) ~15-20%. Independent combination ≈ 1 − (0.93 × 0.93 × 0.82) ≈ 0.29-0.32. That is meaningfully below the agent's 47%.

I'm calling this a warn rather than a fail because: (1) my own estimate carries real spread given the Mistral wildcard and TracXn dating lags, (2) the direction is at least correct (below coin-flip), and (3) the anchor divergence is modest in isolation. But the agent should move decisively below its anchor — into the low-to-mid 30s — rather than parking near the boom-era historical rate. The agent's own Factor 3 (logit −0.7) and the observed May/June zero-entry evidence both point down and are being under-weighted.

Positives: the counterargument is genuinely two-sided and constructs a real alternative story (hyperactive fundraisers + tranche noise → 0.55-0.60 vs. rounds-just-closed → 0.35), not a token gesture. Confidence of 0.60 is well-calibrated given the AskNews credit gap and Mistral timing uncertainty.
